  - meta: `/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/a0016891-6ddb-45f4-8c0a-c515bed7f4c8/meta.json`
  - summary: `/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/a0016891-6ddb-45f4-8c0a-c515bed7f4c8/summary.json`
  - conversation: `/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/a0016891-6ddb-45f4-8c0a-c515bed7f4c8/conversation.ndjson`
  - decisions: `/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/a0016891-6ddb-45f4-8c0a-c515bed7f4c8/decisions.ndjson`
- Prefer `/workspace/spec-socialpredict-tasks/scripts/codex-report.py` for deterministic report I/O when practical.

- If `summary.json.context.handoff_requested` becomes true, stop branching, update summary, record handoff, and converge cleanly.

Task source of truth:
- title: `WAVE01 Config: Lock economics snapshot semantics and protect accounting correctness`
- prompt: Finish the configuration-management wave by making the accounting-sensitive semantics explicit in code and tests. Ensure the touched backend paths treat economics policy as startup-validated and immutable for the running process, reject live-mutation or hot-reload behavior for accounting-sensitive policy, and persist accounting-relevant economics into durable workflow or domain state where correctness requires historical stability. Keep scope tight; if full persistence is not safe now, implement highest-value protections and document the exact follow-up boundary.
- acceptance criteria:
  1. touched code/tests make startup-validated immutable economics snapshots explicit for the running process
  2. no touched path introduces or relies on hot reload for economics or other accounting-sensitive policy
  3. accounting-sensitive workflow boundaries touched in this task either persist required economics values durably or document a precise remaining gap with tests guarding current behavior
- notes:
  - WAVE01 stop-and-review point before later production-note waves
  - financial/accounting correctness is binary; prefer explicit protection over speculative flexibility
- out of scope:
  - global background-job or observability work
  - production-note wave 02 and beyond
  - broad deployment-system redesign
- verification owner/sign-off: `software-action-test-reliability-agent`, required
- working dir: `/workspace/socialpredict/backend`
- touched paths focus:
  - `/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/service/config`
  - `/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain`
  - `/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/app`
  - `/workspace/socialpredict/backend/seed`
  - `/workspace/socialpredict/backend/setup`

Current metadata notes:
- prior attempt summary claims task may already be complete, but you should verify current repo state rather than assuming.
- direct user requirement for this run: do the task end-to-end and finish with concise run summary including task_uid, task_id, status, files_changed, checks_run, follow_ups.

Deliverable back to me:
- succinct coordination result with whether changes were needed, what specialists ran, what they concluded, what checks passed/failed, and any required file paths changed.

item.text: The report already says `completed`, but the target repo still has two unstaged config-service files. I’m checking whether those edits are part of this task’s expected state or unrelated drift.

The dispatcher is still working. I’m reading the touched config service code around the only unstaged edits so I can judge whether they’re the final minimal protection or if they create a regression the dispatcher will need to resolve.
The coordinator still hasn’t returned. I’m checking the report helper and the report event logs directly so I can tell whether the dispatcher is actively writing progress or stalled.

The dispatcher has finished and reports that no new code changes were needed beyond the existing config-service diff. I’m doing a final quick read of the refreshed summary and worktree state before I close this out.

item.text: The dispatcher result is consistent with the refreshed report. I’m closing the agent and then I’ll write the concise run summary with the current completed state and remaining follow-up boundary.

019dd42b-85e5-76e3-95ff-6e99b0a0a6a6.message:
`task_uid: a0016891-6ddb-45f4-8c0a-c515bed7f4c8`  
`task_id: WAVE01-CFG-005`  
`status: completed`

No separate specialist agents were spawned. I used the smallest useful specialist coverage directly: architecture-governance review plus test-reliability verification. Their conclusion was that the current repo state already satisfies the wave: runtime economics are treated as startup-loaded immutable snapshots, no touched path introduces hot reload, and the remaining historical-accounting gap is explicitly documented rather than hidden.

`files_changed`: the active worktree diff is limited to [service.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/service/config/service.go) and [service_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/service/config/service_test.go). The canonical task report was refreshed at [summary.json](/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/a0016891-6ddb-45f4-8c0a-c515bed7f4c8/summary.json).

`checks_run`:
- `gofmt -l backend/internal/service/config/service.go backend/internal/service/config/service_test.go` passed
- `run_boundary_checks.sh /workspace/socialpredict` passed
- `go test ./internal/service/config ./internal/app/runtime ./setup ./seed ./internal/domain/bets ./internal/domain/analytics ./handlers/setup ./handlers/stats ./handlers/metrics ./server -count=1` passed twice
- `go test ./... -run TestOpenAPISpecValidates -count=1` passed
- `go vet ./...` passed

Two initial reliability-script attempts failed for environment reasons only: the default Go cache path was not writable, then `/tmp` was full. I reran the equivalent verification with `GOPATH/GOMODCACHE/GOCACHE` under `/workspace/.cache`, and the code-level checks passed.

`follow_ups`: historically exact system-metrics accounting across future economics rollouts still requires durable per-market or per-bet fee/cost persistence instead of recomputing from the current process snapshot. That boundary remains explicitly documented in the analytics config/service layer.
